name: Basic Risotto and Several Variations
recipeIngredient: 1 Large onion, peeled and finely diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick through the rice to remove any stones or foreign matter, but do not wash it before cooking.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over low heat and sauté the onions until translucent, being careful not to allow them to color or brown at all.
3. Add the Arborio rice, stirring to coat with the olive oil, and sauté with the onions to toast each grain of rice for about 7 minutes. This toasting process adds a chewy, al dente quality that attracts many people to risotto.
4. Once the rice is lightly toasted, add the white wine slowly, stirring with a wooden spoon (a wooden spoon is preferable when making risotto as metal spoons tend to cut or injure the grains of rice and can cause constant stirring).
5. Preheat the chicken stock just to boiling point, then keep it ready at stoveside.
6. After the rice has absorbed the white wine and the skillet is nearly dry, add 1 cup of stock, stirring occasionally, and cook over very low heat until the stock is absorbed. Continue adding the stock, 1 cup at a time, until all the stock has been absorbed (adding liquid in stages allows the grains of rice to expand more fully, enhancing risotto's creamy texture).
7. Once all the liquid has been added and the rice is chewy yet fully cooked with a creamy texture, add the butter, Parmesan, and heavy cream. Stir to combine all ingredients and serve immediately.
8. Yield: 4 servings
Variations:
- Sauteed shrimp
- Mixed seafood (such as salmon, shrimp, and scallops) sautéed in olive oil and herbs
- Diced fresh vegetables (asparagus and carrots make a wonderful combination), steamed or sautéed in olive oil
- Fresh green peas in season
- Sautéed mushrooms and herbs
- Roasted Garlic
